title: config.rs: CLI arg parsing with clap
description:
Create crates/jmap-chat-egui/src/config.rs with clap-derived Config struct.

What: Parse server URL and auth flags from argv into a Config struct.

Algorithm:
1. Define Config struct with #[derive(Parser)] from clap:
   - server_url: String  (#[arg(long, value_name='URL', help='JMAP server base URL')])
   - bearer_token: Option<String> (#[arg(long, value_name='TOKEN')])
   - basic_user: Option<String> (#[arg(long)])
   - basic_pass: Option<String> (#[arg(long)])
   - ca_cert: Option<PathBuf> (#[arg(long, value_name='FILE', help='DER-encoded CA cert')])
2. Add impl Config { pub fn auth_provider(&self) -> Result<Box<dyn AuthProvider>, ClientError> } that constructs the right AuthProvider from flags (BearerAuth > BasicAuth > NoneAuth fallback).
3. Validate: if bearer_token is Some, reject if basic_user/basic_pass also set (ambiguous auth).
4. Add pub fn parse() -> Self { <Config as Parser>::parse() }.

Defensive rules:
- Return ClientError::InvalidArgument on conflicting auth flags, not panic.
- Do not log token value anywhere.
- Path for ca_cert must exist; return error if not.

Tests (in tests/config_test.rs):
- parse_bearer_only: args ['--server-url','http://x', '--bearer-token','tok'] → bearer variant
- parse_basic_only: args [..., '--basic-user','u', '--basic-pass','p'] → basic variant
- parse_no_auth: args ['--server-url','http://x'] → NoneAuth
- parse_conflicting_auth: both bearer and basic → Err(InvalidArgument)
- parse_missing_url: no --server-url → clap error
Oracle: expected variant determined by which flags are supplied, independent of implementation.
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p jmap-chat-egui config exits 0; all 5 config tests pass
